question: Two script task XML snippets are shown above. Which one is evaluated by Zeebe's integrated FEEL engine without any external job worker?

options.text: Either; Zeebe evaluates all script tasks internally regardless of the extension elements used.
Neither; both forms require an external worker to complete the job.

a.text: Snippet 1 uses the inline FEEL form (`zeebe:script` with `expression`), which is evaluated by Zeebe's integrated FEEL Scala engine; no external worker is needed.

b.text: Snippet 2 (`zeebe:taskDefinition` with type `script` plus headers) is the job worker form. Zeebe does not process these jobs; an external worker (such as the community Zeebe Script Worker) must complete them.

c.text: Zeebe only evaluates the inline FEEL form (Snippet 1) internally; the job worker form (Snippet 2) explicitly requires an external worker.

d.text: Snippet 1 does not require a worker — only the job worker form (Snippet 2) does.

explanation: Two script task forms exist. `zeebe:script` with `expression`/`resultVariable` is evaluated by Zeebe's integrated FEEL Scala engine in-broker. `zeebe:taskDefinition type="script"` plus headers is the job worker form and behaves like a service task — Zeebe creates a job and an external worker must complete it.

codeBlocks.stem:
<!-- Snippet 1 -->
<bpmn:scriptTask id="calculate-sum" name="Calculate sum">
  <bpmn:extensionElements>
    <zeebe:script expression="=a + b" resultVariable="sum" />
  </bpmn:extensionElements>
</bpmn:scriptTask>

<!-- Snippet 2 -->
<bpmn:scriptTask id="calculate-sum" name="Calculate sum">
  <bpmn:extensionElements>
    <zeebe:taskDefinition type="script" />
    <zeebe:taskHeaders>
      <zeebe:header key="language" value="javascript" />
      <zeebe:header key="script" value="a + b" />
    </zeebe:taskHeaders>
  </bpmn:extensionElements>
</bpmn:scriptTask>
